_comment: Runtime limits for SAS_Parser. null means 'unset' (the code default applies). Precedence: explicit constructor argument > this file > code default. Loaded via the app_config package; point SAS_PARSER_CONFIG at an alternative file to override.
_vault_comment: Non-secret Vault connection settings only. Secrets (VAULT_TOKEN, VAULT_ROLE_ID, VAULT_SECRET_ID) come from the environment, never this file. address/namespace/auth_path/oidc_role/azure_scopes also read from VAULT_ADDR/VAULT_NAMESPACE/VAULT_AUTH_PATH/VAULT_OIDC_ROLE/VAULT_AZURE_SCOPES, which win over these. Setting oidc_role enables 'azuread' login: an Entra ID JWT (see 'azure' and databricks.secret_scope) is presented to the jwt auth method at auth/<auth_path>/login. ai_gateway_key names the field holding the token in the appsvc/ai_gateway secret; null tries token/api_key/apikey/ai_gateway_token/value in order.
_databricks_comment: Non-secret Databricks workspace settings only. The token (DATABRICKS_TOKEN) and the service-principal secret (ARM_CLIENT_SECRET) come from the environment, never this file; on a cluster no credential is needed, and off-cluster an Entra ID service principal is preferred over a PAT. Every key here is also read from its standard env var, which wins over this file: DATABRICKS_* for the workspace ones, ARM_TENANT_ID/ARM_CLIENT_ID for the service principal (the names Databricks documents), DATABRICKS_AZURE_RESOURCE_ID for azure_workspace_resource_id, DATABRICKS_SECRET_SCOPE for secret_scope. Set azure_workspace_resource_id only when the service principal is NOT assigned to the workspace. secret_scope names a Databricks secret scope holding the service principal under sp-hsv-tenantid/sp-hsv-appid/sp-hsv-secret; reading it needs a credential of its own (a cluster runtime or a PAT).
_azure_comment: Non-secret Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) settings only. The client secret (AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET) comes from the environment, never this file. tenant_id/client_id/authority_host/scopes/certificate_* also read from AZURE_TENANT_ID/AZURE_CLIENT_ID/AZURE_AUTHORITY_HOST/AZURE_SCOPES/AZURE_CLIENT_CERTIFICATE_PATH/AZURE_CLIENT_CERTIFICATE_THUMBPRINT, which win over these. flow: 'client_credentials' (service principal) or 'device_code' (interactive user login); null infers it from the credentials present.
_sharepoint_comment: Non-secret SharePoint (Microsoft Graph) settings only. There is no secret here: authentication is delegated to the 'azure' Entra ID service principal, which mints a Graph token. Identify the site with site_id, or site_hostname + site_path; drive_id targets a specific document library (default: the site's). Every key is also read from its SHAREPOINT_* env var, which wins over this file.
_powerapps_comment: Which SharePoint list the Power Apps conversion requests live in, and the *internal* names of its columns (which often differ from the display names). Used by 'demo_run.py sharepoint'. list_name is required at run time. Column names default to RequestId/ApplicationName/LiveValidation/Model/Timestamp when null. Every key is also read from its POWERAPPS_* env var (POWERAPPS_LIST_NAME, POWERAPPS_<NAME>_FIELD), which wins over this file.
